---
title: Research Areas
description: "My work sits at the intersection of optimization, geometry, and statistics."
toc: true
---

My research lies somewhere at the intersection of optimization, geometry, and statistics. I am particularly interested in both the development and analysis of algorithms for different optimization and statistical tasks defined on non-Euclidean spaces, such as Riemannian manifolds and Wasserstein space. My work is motivated by applications in data science, machine learning, and large-scale optimization.

---

## Riemannian Optimization 
When taught optimization, we are taught many tools and techniques for the following task: 

$$
    \min_{x \in \mathbb{R}^d} f(x),
$$

where $f : \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R}$ is some objective function. This lends itself to many different interpretations, depending on your motivations. For example


## Data Manifolds in Non-Euclidean Spaces


## Discrete Approximations of Continuous Mathematical Objects
